title: The Ugly Duckling (opening excerpt)
author: Hans Christian Andersen
edition: Hans Andersen's Fairy Tales, First Series (English translation)
note: Verbatim opening — from 'It was so beautiful in the country…' through the duckling fleeing the farmyard to the moor ('…feeling very sorrowful.'), preserving source paragraphs as spreads. The full tale is ~3,940 words (over the early-reader ceiling), so this is the complete rejection/outsider arc; the swan-transformation payoff is the tale's ending and is NOT in this excerpt — hence trope 'outsider', not 'transformation'. (For a complete transformation tale at this scale, Kipling's 'The Elephant's Child' fits whole.) A second early-reader fixture alongside aesop, with longer connected narrative.
